prose.rule: DELIVERY is the ONE moment an agent crosses from inside the digital world to the outside real world. It is literalized as the Chief of Agents peeking out of a MacBook: MOSTLY INSIDE the screen, with only its face and hands (holding the finished work) reaching out into the real room to hand it to the human. Legs and most of the body stay inside the laptop. The crossing happens exactly ONCE per delivery; nowhere else does a digital being enter the real world. THE WORK PRODUCT ITSELF BECOMES REAL AT THE CROSSING: the delivered artifact (a bound report, a document, the finished thing) is rendered SOLID, opaque, physical, and casts a real shadow like any real-world object — never holographic, glowing, or orange. The Chief that hands it over is digital and glowing; the work it delivers has landed in the human world and is real. That contrast is the point of the crossing.
prose.source: Locked with Gary 2026-07-16 (chief-delivering-v4, then the isolated delivering.png).
